DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances :
Peter Chao, Esq. 510-832-8899 for Plaintiff (by Zoom).
Bryant Fu and Crystal Lei 415-221-0969 Defendants in Pro Per, (In-person).

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court inquires on an elderly gentleman called Mr. Fu. Defendant Bryant Fu addresses the Court's questions. Plaintiff argues fraud, has new discovery. Defendant responds and mentions first cause of action. The Court shares his views. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRYANT FU, TING POON, CRYSTAL LEI's DEMURRER to 4TH Amended COMPLAINT.

Sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for fraudulent conveyances. By June 23, 2021 ruling Plaintiff was instructed to allege facts regarding non-time barred fraudulent conveyances as to each defendant. Fourth Amended Complaint alleges specific facts as to time barred-conveyances the last of which occurred in 2006. Fourth Amended Complaint makes conclusory statements as to "present" date conveyances in the form of rent (no factual allegations as to the rental property actually generating income in the last 7 years) and amounts in the construction company bank account (no factual allegations that any amounts in the bank accounts were judgment debtor's earnings or income to which judgment debtor is entitled). Applying authorities cited by Plaintiff for the accrual of the cause of action as of the time of the "last over act" in furtherance of the conspiracy, the last properly allege factual assertion of such act dates to 2006, i.e. outside of the statute of limitations. Furthermore, Plaintiff appears to seek re-adjudication of the judgment debtor's assets and earnings between 2003 and 2019. Debtor obtained a discharge in 2019 by representing to the bankruptcy Court that he had no assets and no earnings. Plaintiff is now asking this Court to come to a different determination and find that debtor had assets in his sister's/son's, etc., name during the same time. Any determination as to the fraudulently obtained bankruptcy discharge (including the determination of any of the debtor's assets held by other people) should have been or should be addressed in the bankruptcy Court. The narrow issue that is to be addressed by this court is the existence of any community property shares belonging to the judgment debtor.

Order signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (501/CFH)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANTS BRYANT FU, TING POON and CRYSTAL LEI's DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend. The Court previously ruled on a demurrer to the first amended complaint. Demurrer to the first amended complaint was brought on multiple grounds. To the extent the defendants argued that cause of action for declaratory relief was barred by the statute of limitations, the demurrer was overruled. To the extent the demurrer was based on failure to plead underlying fraud with specificity, the demurrer was sustained with leave to amend. Defendant is demurring to the Second Amended Complaint on the same basis, failure to plead fraud with specificity. Demurrer is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of fraud, including who, to whom, when, how, in what manner made misrepresentations and reliance.
